Mod 4 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 6
Speaker 1: Hey guys and welcome to the workbook for module four. Happy you made it through
the main lesson and now I just want to give a brief recap of those main points and then
we're gonna move into a few exercises and questions that are really going to support
you as you crack out the sequence and write those powerful prelaunch emails that just
really re-shift and reframe your prospect's beliefs. This is really where you're starting to
do that heavy lifting of engineering, that new reality where your product or your offer,
your program makes perfect sense.
So just as recap, the prelaunch sequence will take place right after the confirmation and
lead us straight into your cart open, or that phase of your launch where your program
first becomes available. And this is really that opportunity to cash in on your prospect's
attention and optimism that you earned in module 3 and start moving them through
that series of reframes and a-ha's and attitudinal shifts and insights and beliefs that
invest them further into that journey that really deepen their belief in that outcome and
anchor in the responsibility to act on it.
So what we're going be coaching here, through this sequence are a few things. We're
going to be coaching complete clarity over why the old way wasn't working for them,
and this is what's going to finish that job of relinquishing them of that self blame and
judgment over past failures because now they have full clarity and understanding of it.
It's then going to proceed to coach a new set of empowering and action oriented beliefs
and understandings and attitudes and diagnoses, so we're going to be doing this as it
relates to the opportunity or outcome they're trying to achieve, the problem, past
failures and missteps. This is the big point: why wasn't it their fault? One phrase I see so
much in email marketing is: "It's not your fault." But why wasn't it their fault? You've
got to qualify that. You've got to really break it down in order for that to be something
they can absorb and believe in and internalize and actually act on.
We're gonna also be talking about new beliefs around the more empowering way to
achieve what they're doing and their abilities and capacities to use that way, as well as
your ability to lead them and help them.
And finally, we're going to create some new beliefs and understandings around
competitors that are commonly held conventions, and one way we do this, one way we
really approach this shifting of beliefs and attitudes is by calling out the sacred cows
with sour milk. And once again, these are conventional and commonly held, but
oppressive beliefs that derail the progress of your market. They're the ones most ripe to
be let go of. They're the ones that once they see a new way, deliver that biggest a-ha
because people don't wanna hold onto them. So it's kinda once they get permission to
drop them and see a new more empowering optimistic way, they're so ready to do so
because these are producing the sour milk.
So its kind of like a few examples:
"I can't build an online business until I learn how to use WordPress." Sacred cow,
slaughter it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 6 --

Mod 4 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 6
"I can't play the guitar until I learn how to read sheet music." Once again, if you wanna
play "Stairway to Heaven", here are those chords to do it. You don't need to read sheet
music, and excuse my total lack of guitar knowledge in explaining that.
"In order to have a six figure business I need to have a massive list." What if you don't
have a massive list, but you're a master at pay traffic, and you have an amazing offer
that can convert it at a profit.
"I can't learn how to speak French because I'm terrible at memorizing verbs." I've lived
in Canada in Montreal for over thirty years, and I still don't know how to speak French.
I'm probably one of two percent of Montrealers that don't know how to speak French,
and it's cause I gave up when I was eleven years old and I'm like, "I can't memorize these
verbs, so I might as well quit," but that's probably not the most empowering thing. So I
wish someone slaughtered that sacred cow for me twenty years ago.
"I can't meditate because I can't quiet my mind." There might be a new way to go about
it and your product might have that mechanism that overcomes that block and allows
you to slaughter that sacred cow for your customer.
So these are the ones you want to really call out. The oppressed ones that derail their
progress, but yet are so ingrained and hardened and reinforced by the industry, by
society, by whatever it is, cause those are the ones that are gonna be the most
rewarding for your customer to have release for them, and also the most compelling
and most interesting for them see, the most engaging for them to see in your emails and
in your videos and whatever other mechanism you're using.
So I did include also a list of subject lines, and sometimes it's fun to work backwards on
these. If you're having trouble just brainstorming the sacred cows on their own,
sometimes the subject line surprisingly can be a really good leaping off point, so we use
a lot of these curiosity driven subject lines that imply a diagnosis, and these work
particularly well during the prelaunch. So I'll let you go through these, but I'll just kind
of walk you through them very quickly as well.
"I'll be the First to Admit It." So this is you. Any kind of confession, maybe you used to
have that belief in one of those sacred cows, and it wasn't serving you.
"What You May Not Know About ...", insert that opportunity. Keep this between us. So
you're gonna fill them in on a secret, and it might be something that you don't want to
tell everybody right away because it's kind of that new alternative, it's a sacred cow, and
it might ruffle some feathers.
"Why 'blank' doesn't Work", and in that blank it would be one of those sacred cows.
"Why Memorizing Verbs doesn't Work." For serious, that's just fun and quirky.
"Five Reasons You're Still Experiencing that Problem." Once again, that's very
diagnostic.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

Mod 4 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 3 of 6
"What You Need to Know about 'Topic.'"
"I Can't Believe I Once Believed This." Another entry point into that sacred cow. I can't
believe I once thought I needed to learn how to code to have online business. And this is
serious, this is about six years ago, I was on Udemy trying to learn everything I could
about Ruby on Rails [inaudible 00:07:22], about all these coding languages because I
thought that was an entry to starting an online business.
"Can We Be Honest?" "You Might Want to Sit Down for This." That's kind of a starter.
That's very engaging and implies that you're about to deliver some important
information.
"You Don't Really Believe That, Do You?" I won't be winning any friends. Once again,
when you're calling out a sacred cow that a lot of people hold dear, but your market
would be ready to let go, you might not be the most popular person in the world.
"Can I Tell You a Secret?" "Why isn't Everyone Talking about This?" I swiped this one
from somewhere. I remember it just kind of immediately grabbed me -- "Why isn't
Everyone Talking abut This?" It has that sense of missing out and being left behind, as
well, so that's a really great one to just kind of use a jumping off point.
"The Real Reason 'X.'" We see this a lot. The real reason. And that's another great
jumping off point for talking about sacred cows.
"Can't Believe People Still Believe 'This.'" "I Wish I Learned 'This' Sooner." "Do You Really
Still Believe 'This?'" "Really? In 2017?"
"Three Unexpected Reasons to ... " And that "to" could be the new way of doing things.
It could be just approaching your market. "Three Unexpected Reasons to Burn Your Verb
Book." Whatever it is.
"Why You Haven't Been Able to ... " And the outcome. "Three Reasons Why."
So these are just things to experiment. Take any one of these and just use that as a
jumping off point if you're having trouble brainstorming your sacred cows. We are also
going to get into another exercise in just a bit to help you out with those.
So these are the questions to explore. Once again, you've seen these already in the 47
Questions workbook. If you did them there--great--bring them over and revisit them,
see if you have anything to add. You probably unpacked some additional insights in the
last module, so you will want to give it a second look if you did do it before. If you
haven't, this is chance to really invest yourself in these questions and really extract the
best possible content for this subsection.
Question 1: What about your market struggles breaks your heart?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

Mod 4 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 4 of 6
And it's that breaking-your-heart question that really just cuts through so much
mentation and into that feeling ness of why you do what you do.
What reality do you see for them that they don't yet see for themselves? And this is that
classic frustration you feel when you see a friend or family member or spouse or partner
or parent or child with so much potential and so much ability that they don't yet see for
themselves. And you just kind of wanna, just like do it for them, do it with them. So it's
that same thing, but with your customer and what pains your most about their inability
to see that potential.
This is where we're really starting to ramp up that FOMO, our own fear of them missing
out. And what's the biggest excuse your avatar makes for having not achieving a
favorable outcome yet? So we're not just countering objections later in the closing
sequence. We want to know what their objections and their excuse is for having not
achieved a favorable outcome yet are now.
And more importantly, if we can justify that excuse and validate it, that builds a lot of
trust, especially if we can follow that up with something about you or your method that
overcomes that limitation. If we could isolate that excuse and validate it for them in the
prelaunch, once we introduce our method that overcomes that limitation, there's this
sense of causality. And I know I'm using the word 'loops' a lot, but there's that causality
loop. If we could say, "You haven't achieved it because of 'X,"--an excuse they're already
making, and saying, "Yes. That's very true. That's valid." And then later close it and make
that excuse non valid because of something in your product. That's what creates that
loop and informs them that there's no longer a reason to be limited.
How do your biggest competitors fall short in overcoming it? That's gonna be for your
positioning. And what's a false belief your prospect has about the industry, about
themselves or their ability to achieve it? Maybe about the best way to solve the
problem or achieve the outcome, and why might it be a good thing they've waited until
now? This is always one of my favorite ones to play around with.
And then we are going to get to those sacred cows. This is where you want to identify
them. These are going to be, kind of that crux of your prelaunch sequence. If you're
doing videos, you're going to address either one or more of these in each video. If you're
doing email only, like we're going to show you later, sometimes I'll dedicate one email
to each one of these beliefs--coaching a new belief, coaching release of a sacred cow--in
each email.
It doesn't have to go in this order. You can play around with them, but generally
speaking, as a starting point, I like to have three of them. I like to have one that relates
to the outcome on a macro level. Example: "It's impossible to make a living as a yoga
teacher. You need a big email list to have a successful online business." So we're really
starting macro.
Then we get a little more zoomed into the obstacle and problem. Writing copy is a hard
and painful process. A lot of people believe that. And for a lot of people, it's true, it is,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

Mod 4 - Workbook Walkthrough (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 5 of 6
but does it have to be? That's something we can question. "I'm a yoga teacher. I
shouldn't have to sell. I just want to serve. Shouldn't I just trust that the people I'm
meant to serve will find me?" So that's about their experience in trying to become a well
compensated yoga teacher.
And then we get even more micro and more granular to their abilities to achieve that
outcome. "I'm terrible at the techy stuff. There's no way I can manage half a dozen
social media accounts and all the other business things I need to do. Can't someone just
do that for me?" So that could be a sacred cow for someone who hasn't really been
initiated to online business, sees people doing all these things and thinks they need to
do it all, and that's not something they're good at or want to do, either.
Those sacred cows are going to really be the crux of it, and then you want to follow that
up with the reversals or reframes or a-has that counter them. To continue on that
example, a-ha number one would be, "You're right! You can't make a living as a yoga
teacher, but you can make an amazing living and have a great lifestyle as a yoga
entrepreneur. Would you be open to shifting your definition?" That's more of a
paradigm level shift, a view shift.
How exactly are you being of service when you're stressing out about how you're going
to cover next month's rent? Would it be possible that true service doesn't require
endless self-sacrifice, but a more harmonious and synergistic relationship with money
and the business side? So that's how we would counter that "I don't want to sell" schpiel
that a lot of yoga teachers and just people in that industry feel around it.
And then the third a-ha in this case would be, while being on every single social medial
channel may be needed for a yoga celebrity, its by no means necessary to have a
thriving career as a yoga entrepreneur. So, slaughtering that sacred cow. Much like your
practice, running a successful business doesn't rely on a hundred different poses, but
precision, mindfulness, and consistency on the few that truly serve you and help you
grow. So just kind of using a familiar metaphor for them to really drive in that new a-ha
and insight. So you slaughter that sacred cow and you replace it immediately with that
reversal, that reframe, that a-ha, that empowers them and invigorates them and
energizes them to continue on that journey and just adds to this new layer of
believability. Whenever we see things in a new way, we also simultaneously let go of
some doubt. When we let go of baggage, we kind of can rise a little more, and just kind
of get more perspective and more clarity and more encouragement of what's to come.
So that's it. I want you to go through that exercise. I want you to jot down at least three
sacred cows and the reframe, the associated reframe that is really going to fuel this
journey and give them that spark and give them that renewed hope, optimism,
excitement, and--in this case--by the end of the prelaunch, total believability about the
outcome to come.
That's it. We've got two theme plates to support this. You're gonna need to have
completed this exercise before we get into them. But once you've done that, meet us
back for theme plate number 6, which is gonna be Slaughter House Five, which is gonna
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

be the one you're gonna want to look at if you're doing that minimum viable funnel. And
then we have theme plate number seven, which is going to be Slaughter House Four, so
a smaller variation if you're going to be pointing your prospect to a video or something
else that delivers the reframe.
That's it for here. I will see you in the next video.
